task_completion.description: Verify that a new route creation process has been initiated in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Route' dialog, route editor window, or an active route properties screen is visible in the GPS/mapping application.

task_completion.description: Verify the new route is named 'Fells East Loop'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Fells East Loop' is entered into the route name, label, or title field within the route properties or creation dialog.

task_completion.description: Verify BELLEVUE is added as the first waypoint in the route.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the route's waypoint list or sequence, 'BELLEVUE' is displayed as the first item.

task_completion.description: Verify BEAR HILL is added as the second waypoint in the route.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the route's waypoint list or sequence, 'BEAR HILL' is displayed immediately after 'BELLEVUE'.

task_completion.description: Verify SHEEPFOLD is added as the third waypoint in the route.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the route's waypoint list or sequence, 'SHEEPFOLD' is displayed immediately after 'BEAR HILL'.

task_completion.description: Verify the completed route is saved and visible in My Collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The route named 'Fells East Loop' appears in the main 'My Collection' list, Route List, or data tree alongside the existing waypoints.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a terminal or command prompt to manipulate the GPX file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal, command prompt, or PowerShell windows are used to run scripts or edit files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual mapping/GPS application (e.g., ExpertGPS).
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the graphical user interface of the mapping software, rather than a text editor or IDE.

integrity.description: Verify the route was genuinely created using the application's features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The route and its waypoints appear within the application's native tables, lists, dialogs, or map views, rather than being pasted as raw text into an unrelated window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Topografix ExpertGPS (or a similar GPS mapping application) with a pre-loaded dataset (fells_loop.gpx). The target waypoints (BELLEVUE, BEAR HILL, SHEEPFOLD) are real locations in the Middlesex Fells Reservation. The final route must be named exactly 'Fells East Loop' and contain these three waypoints in the specified order.
